_meta.license: Original illustrations — may be used freely.
_meta.notes: Short sermon/teaching illustrations keyed to passages in Proverbs. Each tells a brief story or analogy and ties back to the verse. Edit freely in TheTruth.

items.title: The Cracked Foundation
items.text: A builder once boasted that he could finish a house in half the time of his rivals. He skipped the soil tests and poured the slab on a Friday. Within two years, hairline cracks ran through every wall. Wisdom is the same: every chapter we try to build without the fear of the LORD develops fault lines we cannot see until the rain comes. The fear of the LORD is not the upstairs decoration; it is the slab everything else is poured on.

items.title: Digging for Silver
items.text: When silver was discovered in the Comstock Lode, men sold their farms and crossed continents to dig. They moved tons of rock for an ounce of metal. Solomon says wisdom is like that — but most of us treat it like loose change. Cry for it. Lift up your voice. Search as for hidden treasure. The promise is not that the LORD will hand it to the curious, but that He will be found by the desperate.

items.title: The Pilot's Instruments
items.text: A small-plane pilot in fog has two choices: trust his inner ear or trust his instruments. The inner ear lies. Pilots who trust it spiral into the ground every year. Trust the instruments and you live. 'Lean not on your own understanding' is not anti-intellectual; it is anti-suicidal. Your gut, in the fog of life, will fly you into a mountain. The Word is the instrument panel. Read it. Obey it. Land safely.

items.title: The First Slice
items.text: A grandmother used to cut her pie and slide the first piece onto a separate plate before anyone sat down. 'That one is the Lord's,' she'd say. The grandkids learned generosity not from a sermon but from a kitchen knife. Honor God with the first, not the leftover. The barn fills when the offering comes off the top.

items.title: The Watershed
items.text: On the Continental Divide a single raindrop can fall on either side of a ridge a few feet wide; one side flows to the Pacific, the other to the Atlantic. The heart is that ridge. What you guard there determines which ocean your whole life flows into. Don't guard the words and ignore the watershed.

items.title: Your Own Well
items.text: In ancient villages every family dug its own well. To drink from a neighbor's cistern was to confess your own had run dry — a public shame. Solomon uses that picture for sexual fidelity. The world says variety is freedom; God says faithfulness is the deepest pleasure. Tend your own well, and rejoice in the wife of your youth.

items.title: The Ant Has No Foreman
items.text: Watch an anthill for ten minutes. No supervisor blows a whistle. No clipboard. Each ant simply works. Solomon does not send the sluggard to a productivity coach — he sends him to a bug. The lesson is not how to do more; it is how to need no one to make you do anything. Initiative is wisdom in work clothes.

items.title: The Lattice Window
items.text: Solomon doesn't preach the dangers of adultery from a pulpit; he opens a window and tells you to look. A young man crosses the street near her house at twilight. He's not yet sinning — he's positioning. Most falls are not slips; they are walks taken with eyes half-closed. Wisdom is choosing a different street long before the lattice window appears.

items.title: She Stands at the Crossroads
items.text: Wisdom is pictured not as a hermit in a cave but as a woman shouting at the busiest intersection in town. The problem is not that wisdom is hard to find; it is that we walk past her with earbuds in. She is loud. She is public. She is calling your name today. The question is whether you'll pull the headphones out.

items.title: Two Banquets
items.text: Imagine walking down a street with two open doors. From one comes the smell of slow-roasted lamb — Wisdom has set her table. From the other, perfume and laughter — Folly is hosting too. Both call. Both promise satisfaction. One feeds you for a lifetime; the other leaves bones in the basement. Every day you choose a doorway. The smell tells the truth before you taste.

items.title: Two Headstones
items.text: Walk through any old cemetery. Some graves are tended every spring; others swallowed by weeds. The difference rarely tracks with wealth or fame. The memory of the righteous is blessed because it bears fruit beyond the casket — a grandchild still quoting their faith, a neighbor still telling their kindness. Build a name worth tending.

items.title: The Thumb on the Scale
items.text: A merchant once kept two sets of weights — heavy ones for buying, light ones for selling. He grew rich and died respected. But Proverbs says the LORD weighs the scales themselves. Heaven is not fooled by clever margins. Whether you sell wheat, hours, or words, the scale you use on others is the scale by which you'll be measured.

items.title: Words Like Surgery
items.text: There is a difference between a butcher's cleaver and a surgeon's scalpel. Both cut, but one heals. Many people speak the truth like a butcher — accurate, efficient, and bloody. The wise speak the same truth like a surgeon: precise, careful, with healing in view. Sharpness is not the issue; aim is.

items.title: The Five You Walk With
items.text: It has been said you become the average of the five people you spend the most time with. Solomon said it first, and harder: walk with the wise and become wise; companion of fools suffers harm. Audit your circle. Not to judge them — to know what you are slowly turning into.

items.title: The Smile in the Photograph
items.text: A pastor visited a family for Christmas dinner. Everyone smiled for the photo. Three months later the marriage was over. Even in laughter the heart may ache. Don't envy other people's highlight reels; pray for them. And don't pretend yourself — take the ache to the One who reads hearts past the smile.

items.title: Lowering the Voice
items.text: A husband and wife realized that every fight in their marriage escalated when one of them raised their voice. So they made a rule: when emotions rise, voices drop. Within a month, the fights were gone. Not the disagreements — the fights. A soft answer is not weakness; it is the off-ramp God gives you to take before the wreck.

items.title: The Detour
items.text: Driving through Tennessee, a family hit a closed bridge they had no idea about. The detour added two hours — and a chance encounter with a stranded driver who later became a lifelong friend. The heart plans the route; the LORD lays the road. Hold your plans like maps, not like demands. The detour is sometimes the destination.

items.title: The 2 A.M. Phone Call
items.text: Everyone has friends for sunny days. The test of a friend is who answers the phone at 2 A.M. when the diagnosis comes back, when the marriage cracks, when the child is in the ER. A brother is born for adversity. Be that friend long before adversity comes — you will not be conscripted then if you have not enlisted now.

items.title: The Half-Read Email
items.text: A manager once fired an employee over an email she'd only half read. The bottom paragraph contained the explanation. She apologized for a year. Folly answers what it has not heard. Wisdom listens to the bottom paragraph before reaching for the keyboard. Speed is not the same as clarity.

items.title: The Wedding Guest List
items.text: Wealth multiplies friends; poverty audits them. A man who lost his business in 2008 said the most painful surprise was who stopped calling. The fewer who remained were treasure. If God ever lets your barns shrink, thank Him for the friend list He clarifies. The poor man's friends are real ones.

items.title: The Mocker in the Glass
items.text: Alcohol does not introduce sins; it removes the brakes from the ones already there. Solomon does not call wine immoral — he calls it a mocker. It will laugh at you while it lowers your guard. Whoever is led astray by it is not wise. Know what is in the glass, and know what is in your heart, before you mix the two.

items.title: The Channeled River
items.text: Farmers in dry country dig channels and the river flows where they direct it. To us a king's heart looks like a wild river — unpredictable, powerful, dangerous. To God it is a channel He is digging. Pray for your leaders not because they are good, but because the One who turns the river is.

items.title: The Bent Sapling
items.text: An arborist trains a young tree by tying it to a stake while the trunk is still pliable. Wait too long and the lean is permanent. The years of training a child are short. Use them. Not to produce a clone, but to set the trunk — so when storms come, the tree leans toward the light.

items.title: The Reference Letter
items.text: Years after a job is done, a reference letter is still being read. A good name is the longest reference letter you will ever write — written one decision at a time, signed by your habits. Riches buy a house; a good name opens doors no key fits.

items.title: The Morning After
items.text: Solomon writes the most vivid anti-drinking passage in Scripture not from outside the bar but inside the headache. He describes the bruises you don't remember getting and the man who wakes up saying, 'When shall I awake? I will seek another drink.' Sin always edits its trailer; God shows you the whole movie.

items.title: Seven Falls
items.text: A toddler learning to walk falls dozens of times in an hour. We do not call her a failure; we call her a walker. The righteous fall seven times and rise. The defining mark is not the fall — it is the rise. Stay down and you have made the fall final. Get up and the fall becomes a step.

items.title: Bread for the Enemy
items.text: A woman whose neighbor had spread rumors about her baked the neighbor a loaf of bread every Saturday for a year. By Christmas the neighbor was weeping on her doorstep. Heap coals of fire — not to burn, but to soften. Kindness is the only weapon hatred has no defense against.

items.title: Starve the Fire
items.text: A campfire dies the moment you stop feeding it. Gossip is the same. Walk into a room where it is burning and refuse to add a stick — the conversation will fizzle within minutes. Where there is no whisperer, contention quiets. You don't have to put the fire out. Just stop feeding it.

items.title: Sparks in the Dark
items.text: Two blades pressed against each other throw sparks. It looks like damage but is actually edge. We tend to think a sharpening friend is a flattering one; usually it is the one who asks the question we were avoiding. Find the friend who throws sparks, not the one who only hands you compliments.

items.title: Know Your Flock
items.text: A shepherd who manages from the porch loses sheep. Whether the flock is your children, your team, or your church, knowing the state means walking among them, learning names, noticing limps. Riches are not forever; presence is the dividend that compounds.

items.title: The Hidden Crack
items.text: An engineer once tried to hide a hairline crack in a beam with paint. The beam held — until it didn't. Hidden sin is exactly that crack. Cover it and you may pass the inspection of men, but the load is coming. He who confesses and forsakes finds mercy. The painted-over beam finds the hospital.

items.title: Without a Map
items.text: A hiker without a map will walk in circles for miles, certain he is making progress. Without revelation, people do the same with their lives. Energy without direction is just sweat. Vision — the Word of God read, believed, and obeyed — is what tells you whether you are climbing toward the summit or wandering further from camp.

items.title: Neither Too Much Nor Too Little
items.text: Agur prays the most underrated prayer in Scripture: give me neither poverty nor riches. Too rich, I'll forget You; too poor, I'll steal. Most people pray for one extreme and ask God to bless it. Agur asks for the boring middle — daily bread — because the middle is where worship survives.

items.title: The Quiet Empire
items.text: Proverbs 31 is not a checklist that crushes wives — it is the portrait a son draws of his mother because she taught him how to see. She is industrious, generous, fearless about tomorrow, and her secret is verse 30: she fears the LORD. Charm fades and beauty fades; reverence builds quiet empires.

items.title: The Last Step
items.text: Climbers say most accidents happen on the descent, not the summit — because by then guard is down. Pride is the descent that follows every accomplishment. The moment you congratulate yourself for not slipping is usually the moment you slip. Walk humbly all the way down the mountain.

items.title: Stronger Than the General
items.text: History remembers the generals who took cities. God remembers the man who took his own temper. Conquering a wall is one battle; conquering yourself is a thousand. Slow to anger is not slow-witted — it is heavyweight strength.

items.title: The Hinge
items.text: A door hinges all day and goes nowhere. The sluggard turns on his bed all night and wakes exhausted. Motion is not progress. Rolling over is not getting up. The first sermon Solomon preaches the lazy is simply: stand up.

items.title: Wings Already on the Bird
items.text: Look at your bank balance long enough and it will sprout wings. Solomon already saw this in 950 B.C. Wealth is a bird, not a building. Build with it; don't worship it. The man who treats his money as if it might fly tomorrow is rarely surprised when it does.

items.title: The Soft Shackle
items.text: Debt is the only chain you put on yourself with a smile. The borrower is servant to the lender — a soft shackle, but a shackle nonetheless. Freedom is rarely as expensive as the bondage we choose to avoid the discomfort of waiting.

items.title: The Loving No
items.text: A child runs toward a busy street; a parent shouts and grabs. Love said no. Discipline is not the absence of love — it is love refusing to lie about what is dangerous. He who spares the rod hates his son means he loves his comfort more than the child's life.

items.title: Coals in Your Lap
items.text: Can a man scoop fire into his lap and his clothes not burn? You read it and laugh — of course not. Then we open the laptop, the app, the secret tab, and assume tonight will be the exception. Solomon's question is rhetorical for a reason. Fire is not selective. Stop scooping.

items.title: Honey in the Hospital
items.text: Researchers have used honey for millennia on stubborn wounds; it heals what antibiotics cannot reach. Solomon noticed first: pleasant words are honeycomb — sweet to the soul, healing to the bones. Most people in your life are walking around with wounds. Bring honey to the hospital today.

items.title: Bold as a Lion
items.text: The wicked flee when no one pursues — every footstep behind them sounds like a sheriff. The righteous are bold as a lion because their conscience is clean. Boldness is not bravado; it is the natural posture of the cleared heart. Confess what needs confessing and you will not need to flee anymore.

items.title: The Cliff at the End of the Road
items.text: Some roads look right for the first ninety miles and end at a cliff. The traveler doesn't know until the last mile, when there is no room to turn around. There is a way that seems right to a man, but its end is death. The wise check the end of the road before they choose it; fools admire the pavement.

items.title: Who You Can Correct
items.text: Try to correct a mocker and you'll get hated. Correct a wise man and he'll thank you and grow. The simplest test of whether someone is wise: how do they receive a hard word? The same test works on you. The next correction you receive will tell you which group you're in.

items.title: The Poor Are Not the Punchline
items.text: Whoever mocks the poor mocks the One who made them. The man on the corner with the cardboard sign was knit together in a womb the same way you were. You may not know his story, but the LORD does. And the LORD takes mockery of His image personally.

items.title: The Friend Who Stings
items.text: Iodine on a cut stings; honey from a flatterer feels good. One heals; one hides infection. Faithful are the wounds of a friend. If no one in your life is allowed to wound you, you are surrounded by enemies' kisses and don't know it.

items.title: The Watered Soul
items.text: A farmer who hoards seed has nothing to plant. A man who waters others is watered himself. Generosity does not make you smaller; clutching does. The hand that opens is the hand that fills.

items.title: Inside the Walls
items.text: In ancient cities, when raiders crested the hill, the bell rang and everyone ran for the watchtower. Once the gate slammed shut, the arrows outside no longer mattered. The Name of the LORD is that tower. Most of us pace the wall arguing with the arrows; the wise are already inside.

items.title: Bringing Your Own Wood
items.text: Some people don't find arguments — they import them. As coals are to burning coals and wood to fire, so a contentious man kindles strife. Notice who walks into rooms and the temperature rises. Notice whether you are that person.

items.title: The Camera Always On
items.text: We behave better when we know we're being recorded. Solomon's news flash: you always are. The eyes of the LORD are in every place, beholding the evil and the good. This is not surveillance to dread; it is the steady gaze of a Father who sees what no one else applauds.

items.title: Don't Open the Letter
items.text: A man received a private letter, then read its contents aloud at the next dinner party. He was never trusted with another. Solomon: argue your case with your neighbor, but do not disclose another's secret. Some letters are not yours to read aloud, even if they came addressed to you.

items.title: Out of Season
items.text: Snow in summer would ruin the harvest. So is honor undeserved — it ruins the fool by confirming what should have been corrected. Be careful what you praise. Misplaced applause builds monuments to folly that the next generation has to tear down.

items.title: The Trap That Compliments You
items.text: The fear of man brings a snare. The trap looks like a compliment: 'What will they think? Will they still like me?' Every step you take to manage someone else's opinion is a step into a cage you decorated yourself. Trust in the LORD is the only key.

items.title: Don't Look at the Glass
items.text: Solomon doesn't say 'do not drink' first — he says 'do not look.' Sin enters through the eye long before it enters through the lip. The battle for sobriety, purity, contentment, and peace is fought at the level of the gaze. Look away early; pour out late.

items.title: Hidden on Purpose
items.text: Children love hide-and-seek not because they fear being found but because the seeking is the joy. It is the glory of God to conceal a matter; the glory of kings to search it out. The mysteries of Scripture are not God being stingy; they are God inviting you to play.

items.title: The Gentle Tongue Breaks Bones
items.text: Iron does not break bone; pressure over time breaks bone. A loud rant changes few minds; a soft, persistent appeal changes hearts and even rulers. By long forbearing is a prince persuaded, and a soft tongue breaks the bone. Be soft. Be steady. Be there tomorrow.

items.title: Even a Fool
items.text: Even a fool, when he holds his peace, is counted wise. Silence has saved more reputations than eloquence ever earned. If you cannot think of the right thing to say, the wisest thing is usually to say nothing at all and let the room conclude you are deeper than you are.

items.title: When the Lights Go Out
items.text: When the stage lights are on, the room is full. Cut the power and watch how many remain. Wealth multiplies friends; the rich have many. The friends who stay when the lights go out are the only ones whose names are worth remembering.

items.title: A Feast in Plain Bread
items.text: Two men sat at the same table eating the same bread. One ate it as a chore, one ate it as a feast. The merry heart has a continual feast — not because the menu changed, but because the heart did. Joy is mostly a posture, not a portion.

items.title: The Medicine of Laughter
items.text: Hospitals now run laughter therapy units; physicians prescribe joy. A merry heart does good like a medicine, but a broken spirit drieth the bones. The bones are listening to the spirit. Feed your spirit hope and your skeleton will hold.

items.title: What the Perfume Cannot Cover
items.text: In the ancient world, expensive oil announced you a block before you arrived. Yet Solomon insists a good name is better than precious oil. Cologne fades by lunchtime; reputation outlives the casket. Spend more on the name; less on the bottle.

items.title: Better the Crust
items.text: Better is a dry morsel and quietness than a house full of feasting with strife. A peaceful kitchen with leftovers beats a Thanksgiving table where everyone is trying not to cry. Build peace at home before you build the menu.

items.title: On the Roof
items.text: Better to dwell in a corner of the housetop than with a brawling woman in a wide house. The verse cuts both ways: it warns wives — and it warns the husbands who drove their wives to brawl. Don't make people choose the roof to escape you.

items.title: Don't Withhold Today's Good
items.text: Withhold not good from them to whom it is due, when it is in the power of thine hand to do it. The favor you owe is not made nobler by the wait. Pay the worker, return the call, send the thank-you, fix the wrong — today, while it is still in your hand.

items.title: Take a Different Street
items.text: Enter not into the path of the wicked. Avoid it, pass not by it, turn from it, and pass away. Five verbs because Solomon knows your feet. The way out of temptation is rarely heroic resistance at the door; it is choosing a different street weeks earlier.

items.title: The Free Class You're Skipping
items.text: Wisdom cries in the street, in the chief place of concourse — free admission, no tuition. Most of us pay thousands for advice we already heard once and ignored. The classroom isn't behind a velvet rope; it's at the corner of every conversation. Listen.

items.title: The Crown of White Hair
items.text: The hoary head is a crown of glory if it be found in the way of righteousness. A culture that worships youth misses the wisdom of survivors. Find a senior saint this week and ask one question: 'What do you wish you had known at my age?' Take notes.

items.title: Pounded but Unchanged
items.text: Though thou shouldest bray a fool in a mortar with a pestle, yet will not his foolishness depart from him. Some lessons no consequence can teach. Don't waste your life trying to grind people into wisdom. Pray for them. Live wisely yourself. Sometimes the only sermon they will hear is your peace.

items.title: Two Things Before I Die
items.text: Agur asks for only two things: remove far from me vanity and lies, and give me neither poverty nor riches. The shortest will, the wisest prayer. Most of our death-bed regrets center on those two requests we never made.

items.title: Small but Wise
items.text: Four creatures are little upon the earth but exceeding wise: ants, conies, locusts, spiders. None has size, status, or strength — only foresight, refuge, order, and persistence. You don't need to be great to be wise. You only need the right four habits.

items.title: When the Earth Trembles
items.text: Agur lists what topples a society: a servant who becomes king, a fool with a full belly, a hated woman married, a maid who supplants her mistress. The common thread is power without character. Pray for leaders to be ready before they rise.

items.title: Cold Drink at Harvest
items.text: As the cold of snow in the time of harvest, so is a faithful messenger to them that send him; for he refresheth the soul of his masters. Do what you said you'd do, when you said you'd do it. Reliability is iced tea on a 100-degree day. Be that to your boss, your spouse, your church.

items.title: The Counsel of a Friend
items.text: Ointment and perfume rejoice the heart; so doth the sweetness of a man's friend by hearty counsel. Some advice we pay therapists for could have been gotten over coffee with a friend who fears God. Don't outsource your soul. Cultivate counselors in your circle.

items.title: Where No Counsel Is
items.text: Where no counsel is, the people fall; but in the multitude of counselors there is safety. Don't make the biggest decision of the decade alone in the shower. Get five trusted voices in the room. The cost of pride is paid by the next decade.

items.title: Many Will Boast
items.text: Most men will proclaim every one his own goodness; but a faithful man who can find? Talk is cheap; faithfulness is rare. Be the friend who shows up at the funeral, the elder who keeps the secret, the husband who comes home. The world is starving for found ones.

items.title: Bruises That Cleanse
items.text: The blueness of a wound cleanseth away evil; so do stripes the inward parts of the belly. Sometimes God uses the consequences of sin to purge what no sermon could reach. The bruise is not God's anger — it is His scrubbing. Don't waste the wound by resenting it.

items.title: The Eye That Despises
items.text: The eye that mocketh at his father, and despiseth to obey his mother, the ravens of the valley shall pick it out. Strong language for a culture that smirks at parents. The way you treat the ones who raised you is a leading indicator of how you'll treat the God who made you.

items.title: He That Hasteth
items.text: Without knowledge the soul is not good; and he that hasteth with his feet sinneth. The fastest decision is rarely the best decision. Slow down enough to know what you're doing. Speed is not the same as faith; sometimes it is just panic in a hurry.

items.title: The Discretion to Defer
items.text: The discretion of a man defereth his anger; and it is his glory to pass over a transgression. Not every offense deserves a response. Letting it go is not weakness; it is the glory of strength under control. Choose which hills to die on — most are not worth the climb.

items.title: Built by Wisdom
items.text: Through wisdom is a house builded, by understanding it is established, and by knowledge shall the chambers be filled with all precious and pleasant riches. The architecture of a home is not square footage — it is wisdom in the corners. Knowledge furnishes rooms money cannot buy.

items.title: Don't Cheer the Stretcher
items.text: Rejoice not when thine enemy falleth, lest the LORD see it, and it displease Him. The temptation when an opponent stumbles is to laugh on the inside. God notices the inside. Pray for the fallen, even when the fallen wronged you. Heaven keeps a different scoreboard.

items.title: The Polluted Well
items.text: A righteous man falling down before the wicked is as a troubled fountain, and a corrupt spring. When godly people compromise publicly, the whole town's water gets dirty. Stand for what is right — not for your sake, but for everyone downstream of you.

items.title: A Jewel in a Snout
items.text: As a jewel of gold in a swine's snout, so is a fair woman without discretion. The image is jarring on purpose. Beauty without character is wasted treasure. Marry for character; train your sons to look for it; train your daughters to develop it.

items.title: What Children Make of Parents
items.text: A wise son maketh a glad father, but a foolish son is the heaviness of his mother. Long after report cards, parents read the deeper transcript of how their grown children live. Make your parents glad while you still have time — it is the easiest gift to give and the one most often delayed.

items.title: Money That Cannot Save
items.text: Treasures of wickedness profit nothing, but righteousness delivereth from death. Some people make a fortune and lose their soul; some lose their fortune and find their soul. At the end, righteousness writes the only check the grave will cash.

items.title: What Love Covers
items.text: Hatred stirreth up strifes, but love covereth all sins. To cover is not to deny — it is to refuse to broadcast. Love handles a brother's failure with a blanket, not a megaphone. The next failure you witness will reveal which you reach for first.

items.title: The Slack Hand
items.text: He becometh poor that dealeth with a slack hand, but the hand of the diligent maketh rich. Wealth is rarely a single deal; it is a thousand small acts of follow-through. The slack hand drops opportunities the diligent hand quietly catches.

items.title: Without Added Sorrow
items.text: The blessing of the LORD, it maketh rich, and He addeth no sorrow with it. Wealth gained by compromise comes with a sorrow rider you don't read until it's too late. Wealth from God arrives clean. Pray for the kind whose receipt has no fine print.

items.title: The Substituted Trouble
items.text: The righteous is delivered out of trouble, and the wicked cometh in his stead. There are deliverances we never see — trains we missed, deals that fell through, doors that didn't open. Years later we say 'thank You.' Often, we never know what God spared us from. Trust Him in the no's.

items.title: Trees That Grow People
items.text: The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life, and he that winneth souls is wise. The wisest investment is not a portfolio — it is a person. Souls won outlast bull markets. Plant a tree this year that grows people, not just income.

items.title: The Lip That Lasts
items.text: The lip of truth shall be established forever, but a lying tongue is but for a moment. Lies have an expiration date — short shelf life, public exposure, embarrassing aftermath. Truth, even unpopular truth, outlives every news cycle. Build with words that last.

items.title: When the Tree Comes In
items.text: Hope deferred maketh the heart sick, but when the desire cometh, it is a tree of life. The waiting room is the hardest seat in the kingdom. But God plants oaks in the soil of long patience. Don't pull up the seed because the sprout is slow.

items.title: The Builder and the Wrecker
items.text: Every wise woman buildeth her house, but the foolish plucketh it down with her hands. Houses are not undone by storms half so often as by their occupants. The same hands can lay bricks or pull them; choose every day which the hands will do.

items.title: Mutual Disgust
items.text: An unjust man is an abomination to the just; and he that is upright in the way is an abomination to the wicked. Don't be surprised when the world finds you offensive. The light disturbs the dark on principle. Quiet faithfulness is its own provocation.

items.title: A Place of Refuge for Children
items.text: In the fear of the LORD is strong confidence, and His children shall have a place of refuge. The greatest legacy you can leave your children isn't a trust fund — it is a Father they know they can run to long after you're gone. Build a home that points them upward.

items.title: A Manual for Living
items.text: Every appliance comes with a manual, but most of us only read it after something breaks. Solomon wrote a manual for life and put it up front: wisdom, instruction, understanding, justice, judgment, equity. Read it before things break.

items.title: The First Step Off the Map
items.text: Hikers who refuse the trailhead sign get lost in beautiful country. The fear of the LORD is the trailhead. Skip it and even your cleverness will lead you in circles.

items.title: An Ornament of Grace
items.text: Hear the instruction of thy father, and forsake not the law of thy mother. Children chafe at parental rules until life applies its own. The wise child learns from the lighter rod of love rather than the heavier rod of consequence.

items.title: When They Say Come With Us
items.text: If sinners entice thee, consent thou not. Notice God doesn't say 'if'; He says 'when.' The invitation will come. Decide your answer before the doorbell rings, not in the doorway.

items.title: The Net the Bird Sees
items.text: Surely in vain the net is spread in the sight of any bird. Yet sinners hide their nets in plain sight and we still hop in. Open your eyes; the trap is rarely subtle when you slow down to look.

items.title: She Stands at the Corner
items.text: Wisdom crieth without; she uttereth her voice in the streets. Wisdom is not hiding in a library — she's shouting on the corner. We pass her every day; the question is whether we stop and listen.

items.title: When Heaven Will Not Answer
items.text: I also will laugh at your calamity. There is a season when the door of mercy is open and a season when even cries cannot find ears. The time to call is now, not later.

items.title: Eating Your Own Recipe
items.text: Therefore shall they eat of the fruit of their own way. God's most fearful judgment is sometimes letting us live with our choices. The recipe was ours; the meal will be ours too.

items.title: Dwelling Safely Within
items.text: Whoso hearkeneth unto Me shall dwell safely, and shall be quiet from fear of evil. The world cannot stop bad news from coming, but God can stop bad news from owning your peace. Listen and be still.

items.title: Killed by Comfort
items.text: The prosperity of fools shall destroy them. More souls have been ruined by ease than by hardship. Ask God to keep you tender in the very seasons He keeps you blessed.

items.title: Lean In to Listen
items.text: Incline thine ear unto wisdom. Hearing isn't passive — it's a posture. The wise lean in like a child to a grandfather's whisper. Distracted ears miss the best lines.

items.title: When You Want It Like Air
items.text: If thou criest after knowledge and liftest up thy voice for understanding. Wisdom is given to people who actually want it, not just admire it. Cry for it the way a drowning man cries for breath.

items.title: Mining for Wisdom
items.text: If thou seekest her as silver, and searchest for her as for hid treasures. Miners don't quit at the first dry shaft. Search the Scripture like a man with a pickaxe and a lantern, not a tourist with a postcard.

items.title: The Source, Not Just the Search
items.text: For the LORD giveth wisdom: out of His mouth cometh knowledge and understanding. You don't manufacture wisdom — you receive it. Stop straining for it and start asking the One who gives it freely.

items.title: A Shield Already Strapped On
items.text: He is a buckler to them that walk uprightly. Walk upright and you discover protections you didn't even know were issued to you. Most of God's defenses are invisible until tested.

items.title: The Compass Calibrates
items.text: Then shalt thou understand righteousness, and judgment, and equity. Wisdom doesn't just tell you what to do — it recalibrates the inner compass for the next decision you haven't faced yet.

items.title: Delivered From Bad Company
items.text: To deliver thee from the way of the evil man. Wisdom doesn't just teach you who to be — it teaches you who to leave. Some friendships need not a confrontation but a fade.

items.title: Smooth Words, Slick Floor
items.text: To deliver thee from the strange woman, even from the stranger which flattereth with her words. Flattery is a slick floor; smart men still slip on it. Distrust any voice whose only message is how wonderful you are.

items.title: The Road With No Return
items.text: None that go unto her return again, neither take they hold of the paths of life. Some doors lock behind you. The mercy of God can rescue — but easier is the door you never opened.

items.title: The Footpath of the Faithful
items.text: That thou mayest walk in the way of good men, and keep the paths of the righteous. The trail is clearer when others have walked it ahead of you. Pick mentors whose footprints you'd be glad to land in.

items.title: Inheritance of the Upright
items.text: For the upright shall dwell in the land. Wickedness rents; uprightness inherits. The headlines belong to the loud, but the land belongs to the faithful.

items.title: Two Cords Around Your Neck
items.text: Let not mercy and truth forsake thee: bind them about thy neck. Mercy without truth is sentiment; truth without mercy is cruelty. Wear both like a necklace — always together, always visible.

items.title: Wise Eyes See Themselves Last
items.text: Be not wise in thine own eyes: fear the LORD, and depart from evil. The most dangerous fool is the one who thinks he's the smartest in the room. Bow first; assess later.

items.title: Top of the Pile
items.text: Honour the LORD with thy substance, and with the firstfruits of all thine increase. God doesn't take leftovers as a compliment. Put Him on top of the budget and watch the rest reorder itself.

items.title: Don't Despise the File
items.text: My son, despise not the chastening of the LORD. The carpenter's file looks cruel to the wood, but it's making something useful. God's correction is rarely random; it is rarely wasted.

items.title: Better Than the Markets
items.text: The merchandise of it is better than the merchandise of silver. Wall Street has good days and bad ones; wisdom only appreciates. Invest in the only commodity that survives the next crash.

items.title: A Tree You Can Hold
items.text: She is a tree of life to them that lay hold upon her. Wisdom isn't decoration — it's a tree, and you eat from it. Reach for it daily; it's bearing fruit you'll need before sundown.

items.title: Sleep Sweet
items.text: When thou liest down, thou shalt not be afraid: yea, thou shalt lie down, and thy sleep shall be sweet. Worry is a thief of sleep; obedience is a sedative. Lie down with a clean conscience and the night repays you.

items.title: Don't Hold Back the Help
items.text: Withhold not good from them to whom it is due, when it is in the power of thine hand to do it. Delayed help is half a denial. If you can do it now, the second errand isn't promised tomorrow.

items.title: Don't Envy the Bully
items.text: Envy thou not the oppressor, and choose none of his ways. Bullies often look like winners. Don't trade your soul for their highlight reel; the credits roll differently than the trailer.

items.title: Guard the Wellhead
items.text: Keep thy heart with all diligence; for out of it are the issues of life. Cities have walls and banks have vaults, but most men leave the heart unguarded. Whatever you let in there will run downstream into everything else.

items.title: Hear, Ye Children
items.text: Hear, ye children, the instruction of a father. There is wisdom in your father's mistakes that no textbook contains. Don't pay tuition for lessons you could have audited at the kitchen table.

items.title: The Principal Thing
items.text: Wisdom is the principal thing; therefore get wisdom: and with all thy getting get understanding. We get cars, houses, degrees — but skip the one acquisition that runs everything else. Add wisdom to the cart first.

items.title: Brighter By Noon
items.text: The path of the just is as the shining light, that shineth more and more unto the perfect day. The Christian life is not a flashbulb but a sunrise. If you're walking with God, the noon is brighter than the dawn.

items.title: Stumbling at What
items.text: The way of the wicked is as darkness: they know not at what they stumble. Sin doesn't just trip people — it blinds them to what tripped them. Mercy is light enough to see the curb you keep hitting.

items.title: Health From the Page
items.text: My son, attend to my words... For they are life unto those that find them, and health to all their flesh. The Word is medicine the body absorbs through the soul. Skip a dose and you'll feel it before the week is out.

items.title: Put It Far From You
items.text: Put away from thee a froward mouth, and perverse lips put far from thee. You don't reform crooked speech — you evict it. Some habits won't be tamed; they have to be shown the door.

items.title: Eyes Forward
items.text: Let thine eyes look right on, and let thine eyelids look straight before thee. Drivers crash by drifting their eyes. Lives crash the same way. Pick a fixed point ahead and stop sightseeing the ditch.

items.title: Survey Before You Step
items.text: Ponder the path of thy feet, and let all thy ways be established. Most regrets begin with an unpondered step. Five seconds of thought save five years of cleanup.

items.title: Neither to the Right Nor Left
items.text: Turn not to the right hand nor to the left: remove thy foot from evil. The road is narrow on purpose. Holiness is rarely a question of bad choices; it is usually a question of staying in the lane.

items.title: Honey at the Front, Wormwood at the End
items.text: The lips of a strange woman drop as an honeycomb... but her end is bitter as wormwood. Sin always shows the dessert menu and hides the bill. Read the receipt before you order.

items.title: Feet That Take Hold of Hell
items.text: Her feet go down to death; her steps take hold on hell. The path begins with a smile and ends in a grave. Notice where the road leads, not how the entrance is decorated.

items.title: Don't Walk Past the Door
items.text: Remove thy way far from her, and come not nigh the door of her house. God's strategy with this sin is geography. Don't argue with it; relocate.

items.title: Honor You Cannot Get Back
items.text: Lest thou give thine honour unto others, and thy years unto the cruel. Reputation built over decades can be deleted in a weekend. Spend honor like it's nonrefundable, because it is.

items.title: The Mourner at the End
items.text: And thou mourn at the last, when thy flesh and thy body are consumed. Sin's interview is upbeat; its exit interview is in tears. Listen to the second one before signing the first.

items.title: Your Own Well
items.text: Drink waters out of thine own cistern, and running waters out of thine own well. God gave you a well at home. Stop looking thirsty in front of the neighbor's faucet.

items.title: The Wife of Thy Youth
items.text: Let thy fountain be blessed: and rejoice with the wife of thy youth. Loyalty isn't grim duty — it is supposed to be celebration. Choose joy where you've already vowed love.

items.title: Ravished With Her Love
items.text: Be thou ravished always with her love. Marriage is meant to be a feast, not a famine. Tend the home table and the strange one will lose its appeal.

items.title: Why Settle for the Stranger
items.text: And why wilt thou, my son, be ravished with a strange woman? It is a Father's question, not a sermon point. Ask it in the mirror before the next decision is made.

items.title: The Ways Are Open
items.text: For the ways of man are before the eyes of the LORD, and He pondereth all his goings. Cameras may not be everywhere, but God is. Live each day as if heaven gets the unedited footage — because it does.

items.title: Dying Without Instruction
items.text: He shall die without instruction; and in the greatness of his folly he shall go astray. Some men refuse to be taught and end up taught by the gravestone. Be teachable now while teachers still come gently.

items.title: Snared by a Signature
items.text: My son, if thou be surety for thy friend, if thou hast stricken thy hand with a stranger. A signature can shackle you for years. Read every line; co-sign nothing you can't afford to lose.

items.title: Like a Roe From the Hunter
items.text: Deliver thyself as a roe from the hand of the hunter. If you're caught in a foolish promise, get out fast. Pride says 'finish what you started.' Wisdom says 'flee.'

items.title: No Boss, Still Working
items.text: The ant... having no guide, overseer, or ruler, provideth her meat in the summer. Mature people work without a manager standing over them. If your effort changes when no one's watching, your character is the bottleneck.

items.title: Poverty as a Soldier
items.text: So shall thy poverty come as one that travelleth, and thy want as an armed man. Poverty doesn't always knock politely — sometimes it kicks in the door. Sloth is the unlocked latch that lets it in.

items.title: The Whisperer With the Wink
items.text: A naughty person, a wicked man, walketh with a froward mouth. He winketh with his eyes. The body language tells on people before the words do. Watch the wink, not the smile.

items.title: The List God Hates
items.text: These six things doth the LORD hate; yea, seven are an abomination unto Him. God doesn't roll His eyes at sin — He keeps a list. Read it; live as a man trying to be on the opposite list.

items.title: He Who Sows Discord
items.text: He that soweth discord among brethren. God hates the gossip more than the gossip's juiciest secret. Refuse to be the messenger that splits a family or a church.

items.title: Tie It On
items.text: Bind them continually upon thine heart, and tie them about thy neck. Truths you don't fasten down get loose by Tuesday. Bind God's Word the way a soldier binds his sword — close enough to draw without thinking.

items.title: Fire in Your Coat
items.text: Can a man take fire in his bosom, and his clothes not be burned? You don't flirt with sin and walk away cool. Heat leaves a smell, a scar, a story. Don't carry coals.

items.title: The Husband's Rage
items.text: For jealousy is the rage of a man: therefore he will not spare in the day of vengeance. Adultery wakes a wrath no apology can quiet. The destruction reaches families for a generation; weigh the night against the years.

items.title: The Young Man Void of Understanding
items.text: I discerned among the youths a young man void of understanding. Most ruined men were once curious boys with no anchor. Pour wisdom into the young while their hearts still have soft soil.

items.title: In the Twilight
items.text: In the twilight, in the evening, in the black and dark night. Sin loves dim light. The right friends will check on you when the lights are low and the choices feel private.

items.title: Dressed for the Trap
items.text: There met him a woman with the attire of an harlot, and subtil of heart. The packaging is part of the snare. What you let your eyes find pretty trains what your heart will eventually find acceptable.

items.title: Loud and Stubborn
items.text: She is loud and stubborn; her feet abide not in her house. Notice the diagnostic: a soul that won't stay home. Restlessness is often the engine of sin; tend the home before tending the impulse.

items.title: The Husband Far From Home
items.text: For the goodman is not at home, he is gone a long journey. The bait was 'no one's watching.' But God was. The empty chair is never as empty as it looks.

items.title: She Forced Him With Words
items.text: With her much fair speech she caused him to yield, with the flattering of her lips she forced him. Words are stronger than locks. Guard your ears as carefully as you guard your bedroom door.

items.title: Like an Ox to the Slaughter
items.text: He goeth after her straightway, as an ox goeth to the slaughter. The ox doesn't know it's the last walk. Sin counts on you not knowing either.

items.title: He Knew Not That It Was for His Life
items.text: And knoweth not that it is for his life. The deepest tragedy is sleepwalking into ruin. Pray for awakened eyes before the next decision; ask God to show you the cost in advance.

items.title: Decline Not Into Her Paths
items.text: Let not thine heart decline to her ways, go not astray in her paths. Heart-decline starts before foot-travel. Watch the slope of your affections; the feet will follow what the heart already wants.

items.title: Strong Men, Wounded Bad
items.text: She hath cast down many wounded: yea, many strong men have been slain by her. The strongest fall here. Don't trust your strength; trust the door, locked from the inside.

items.title: Way to Hell
items.text: Her house is the way to hell, going down to the chambers of death. The doormat says welcome; the basement says grave. Believe what God labels, not what culture decorates.

items.title: Wisdom Has a Voice
items.text: Doth not wisdom cry? and understanding put forth her voice? Wisdom is not silent — we are deaf. Tune your ear and discover she has been calling all along.

items.title: Excellent Things
items.text: Hear; for I will speak of excellent things. Wisdom doesn't traffic in trivia. When she speaks, she speaks about what matters. Show up with the right questions and she'll fill your hours.

items.title: Better Than Rubies
items.text: For wisdom is better than rubies; and all the things that may be desired are not to be compared to it. Stack everything you've ever wanted on one side and wisdom on the other; the scale tips for wisdom every time.

items.title: What the Fear of God Hates
items.text: The fear of the LORD is to hate evil: pride, and arrogancy, and the evil way... do I hate. If you say you fear God but love what He hates, you don't fear Him — you tolerate Him.

items.title: Counsel Is Mine
items.text: Counsel is mine, and sound wisdom: I am understanding; I have strength. Don't go to a meeting without first going to Wisdom's office. She has insights the boardroom doesn't.

items.title: By Me Kings Reign
items.text: By me kings reign, and princes decree justice. Behind every just policy is borrowed wisdom from God. Nations rise and fall on whether their rulers learn that.

items.title: Loved by Wisdom
items.text: I love them that love me; and those that seek me early shall find me. Wisdom is not aloof. She returns affection. Seek her early in the day and early in life; she rewards both.

items.title: Riches That Last
items.text: Riches and honour are with me; yea, durable riches and righteousness. Most riches rust; wisdom's riches don't. The dividends of godliness compound for eternity.

items.title: Before the World Was
items.text: I was set up from everlasting, from the beginning, or ever the earth was. Wisdom is older than the mountains. The same Voice that ordered the cosmos can order your week. Ask Him.

items.title: Watching at the Gates
items.text: Blessed is the man that heareth me, watching daily at my gates, waiting at the posts of my doors. Wisdom rewards regulars. Show up at her door each morning and you'll start to look like the company you keep.

items.title: Whoso Findeth Me
items.text: For whoso findeth me findeth life, and shall obtain favour of the LORD. Find wisdom and you find a Person — Christ Himself. To miss Him is to miss life.

items.title: A Feast With Open Doors
items.text: Wisdom hath builded her house, she hath hewn out her seven pillars. The table is set, the bread baked, the wine poured. The only question is who shows up. Refusing the invitation is the worst kind of fasting.

items.title: Reprove a Wise Man
items.text: Reprove not a scorner, lest he hate thee: rebuke a wise man, and he will love thee. The mark of wisdom is not being right — it is loving the person who corrects you. Take the temperature of your reactions.

items.title: Give a Wise Man Instruction
items.text: Give instruction to a wise man, and he will be yet wiser. Wisdom isn't a finish line; it's a compounding interest account. The more you have, the faster it grows when added to.

items.title: The Beginning, Not the Bonus
items.text: The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom: and the knowledge of the holy is understanding. You don't add God to your education; you start there. Skip the beginning and the rest doesn't add up.

items.title: Days Will Be Multiplied
items.text: By me thy days shall be multiplied, and the years of thy life shall be increased. Wisdom doesn't only deepen days; it lengthens them. Folly tends to shorten the calendar even when it hasn't shortened the breath.

items.title: You Bear It Yourself
items.text: If thou be wise, thou shalt be wise for thyself: but if thou scornest, thou alone shalt bear it. No one else gets the bill for your scorn. Pretending the consequences are someone else's is the first move of every fool.

items.title: A Foolish Woman Is Clamorous
items.text: A foolish woman is clamorous: she is simple, and knoweth nothing. Folly is loud because she has nothing to say. Distrust noise; wisdom rarely shouts.

items.title: Stolen Waters Are Sweet
items.text: Stolen waters are sweet, and bread eaten in secret is pleasant. The forbidden tastes better only because of the secrecy, not the substance. Bring the meal into the daylight and the flavor disappears.

items.title: He Knoweth Not
items.text: But he knoweth not that the dead are there; and that her guests are in the depths of hell. The party photos don't show the basement. Sin always crops the consequences out of the picture.

items.title: Calling Passers-By
items.text: To call passengers who go right on their ways. Folly waits at the corner you cross every day. The destination she offers requires no commitment, just a small detour. Stay on your way.

items.title: Choice Silver, Choice Words
items.text: The tongue of the just is as choice silver: the heart of the wicked is little worth. Words from a clean heart ring like fine metal. Words from a corrupt one clatter like tin. Listen to the metal of a man's speech.

items.title: Lips That Feed Many
items.text: The lips of the righteous feed many: but fools die for want of wisdom. Some people open their mouths and a meal comes out for the soul. Be that mouth at your dinner table this week.

items.title: Riches Without the Riders
items.text: The blessing of the LORD, it maketh rich, and He addeth no sorrow with it. Earned-by-compromise wealth always comes with hidden riders. God's wealth has clean paperwork. Wait for His check.

items.title: What He Feared Came
items.text: The fear of the wicked, it shall come upon him. The thing he tried to outrun ran him down. The righteous man's hopes arrive; the wicked man's fears do.

items.title: When the Whirlwind Passes
items.text: As the whirlwind passeth, so is the wicked no more: but the righteous is an everlasting foundation. Storms are the great revealer of who built on rock. The whirlwind is coming; check the foundation now.

items.title: Vinegar to the Teeth
items.text: As vinegar to the teeth, and as smoke to the eyes, so is the sluggard to them that send him. The lazy worker is a small daily ache to those depending on him. Be the kind of help no one regrets sending for.

items.title: Rigged Scales
items.text: A false balance is abomination to the LORD: but a just weight is His delight. God notices a thumb on the scale even when the customer doesn't. Cheat in pennies and you'll lose in pounds.

items.title: When Pride Comes
items.text: When pride cometh, then cometh shame: but with the lowly is wisdom. Pride and shame travel as a pair; they always check into the same hotel. Send pride home before its companion arrives.

items.title: Integrity as a Guide
items.text: The integrity of the upright shall guide them: but the perverseness of transgressors shall destroy them. Honest people don't need a map for every situation; integrity is its own GPS.

items.title: By the Mouth of the Wicked
items.text: By the blessing of the upright the city is exalted: but it is overthrown by the mouth of the wicked. One godly voice can lift a town; one wicked voice can sink it. Use yours like it's the swing vote.

items.title: The Talebearer
items.text: A talebearer revealeth secrets: but he that is of a faithful spirit concealeth the matter. Some friends keep secrets like a bank vault; others, like a sieve. Be the vault.

items.title: Many Counselors
items.text: Where no counsel is, the people fall: but in the multitude of counsellors there is safety. Big decisions deserve more than one voice. Loneliness in leadership is often the first sign of trouble.

items.title: Love the Correction
items.text: Whoso loveth instruction loveth knowledge: but he that hateth reproof is brutish. Your reaction to correction is a more accurate IQ test than any school exam. Cherish the friend who tells you the truth.

items.title: A Good Man Obtaineth Favour
items.text: A good man obtaineth favour of the LORD. Goodness is not extinct; God still notices and still rewards. Don't trade decades of quiet good for one loud compromise.

items.title: A Crown to Her Husband
items.text: A virtuous woman is a crown to her husband: but she that maketh ashamed is as rottenness in his bones. The right wife elevates a man like jewelry on a head; the wrong wife corrodes from inside. Choose carefully; honor faithfully.

items.title: Even the Thoughts
items.text: The thoughts of the righteous are right: but the counsels of the wicked are deceit. Righteousness shows up in the imagination first. Watch what you rehearse in private; it's auditioning for your public.

items.title: A House That Stands
items.text: The wicked are overthrown, and are not: but the house of the righteous shall stand. Headlines tell us who's winning today; obituaries tell us who won. Build a house that survives the news cycle.

items.title: Even His Beast
items.text: A righteous man regardeth the life of his beast: but the tender mercies of the wicked are cruel. Watch how a man treats his dog and you'll know how he treats his employees. Kindness is not selective in the godly.

items.title: Till Your Land
items.text: He that tilleth his land shall be satisfied with bread: but he that followeth vain persons is void of understanding. Tend to the dirt in front of you. Get-rich schemes are the loudest sales pitch in the universe and the worst-performing fund.

items.title: Filled by His Words
items.text: A man shall be satisfied with good by the fruit of his mouth. Your words come back to feed you. Speak life today; eat from that orchard for years.

items.title: Right in His Own Eyes
items.text: The way of a fool is right in his own eyes: but he that hearkeneth unto counsel is wise. The fool's mirror always tells him he's correct. The wise man asks a witness.

items.title: Piercings of a Sword
items.text: There is that speaketh like the piercings of a sword: but the tongue of the wise is health. Some sentences cut for years. Choose words like a surgeon, not a swordsman.

items.title: A Wise Son Hears
items.text: A wise son heareth his father's instruction: but a scorner heareth not rebuke. The same words land differently on different hearts. Soften the soil before you sow the seed.

items.title: He That Keepeth His Mouth
items.text: He that keepeth his mouth keepeth his life: but he that openeth wide his lips shall have destruction. Half of life's wreckage comes from sentences spoken five seconds too soon. Practice the holy pause.

items.title: The Soul of the Diligent
items.text: The soul of the sluggard desireth, and hath nothing: but the soul of the diligent shall be made fat. Wishing is not working. The diligent man's prayer has hands attached.

items.title: The Light of the Righteous
items.text: The light of the righteous rejoiceth: but the lamp of the wicked shall be put out. Some lamps go out at the end; others burn brighter. The fuel of righteousness is inexhaustible.

items.title: Only by Pride
items.text: Only by pride cometh contention: but with the well advised is wisdom. Most fights are not about the topic; they are about the ego. Lay pride down and the argument has nothing to grip.

items.title: Wealth That Lasts
items.text: Wealth gotten by vanity shall be diminished: but he that gathereth by labour shall increase. Easy money goes out the way it came in. Hard-earned money grows roots.

items.title: Good Understanding Giveth Favour
items.text: Good understanding giveth favour: but the way of transgressors is hard. The wise man's road has hills; the fool's road has cliffs. Pick the harder slope, not the harder fall.

items.title: Walking With the Wise
items.text: He that walketh with wise men shall be wise: but a companion of fools shall be destroyed. You become the average of your closest five. Audit your circle as carefully as you'd audit your bank account.

items.title: He That Spareth
items.text: He that spareth his rod hateth his son: but he that loveth him chasteneth him betimes. Discipline isn't the opposite of love — it's evidence of it. Painful in the moment, treasured at the wedding.

items.title: Mercy on the Poor
items.text: He that despiseth his neighbour sinneth: but he that hath mercy on the poor, happy is he. Joy hides in unexpected places — mostly in pockets opened to others. Find the next person you can quietly bless.

items.title: Talk Versus Toil
items.text: In all labour there is profit: but the talk of the lips tendeth only to penury. Mouths can outpace muscles by a country mile. Less posting, more producing.

items.title: Slow to Wrath
items.text: He that is slow to wrath is of great understanding: but he that is hasty of spirit exalteth folly. Hot tempers may win the argument and lose the relationship. Cool heads keep both.

items.title: A Sound Heart
items.text: A sound heart is the life of the flesh: but envy the rottenness of the bones. Envy doesn't just steal joy; it eats the body. Practice gratitude as preventive medicine.

items.title: Righteousness Exalteth a Nation
items.text: Righteousness exalteth a nation: but sin is a reproach to any people. Nations don't fall because of weak armies but rotted morals. Pray for righteousness, vote for it, live it.

items.title: The Poor Is Hated
items.text: The poor is hated even of his own neighbour: but the rich hath many friends. Real friendship is tested when your bank account dips. Be the friend who sticks when the dollars don't.

items.title: The Simple Believeth Every Word
items.text: The simple believeth every word: but the prudent man looketh well to his going. Forwarded headlines and viral videos are not evidence. Verify before you amplify.

items.title: A Soft Answer
items.text: A soft answer turneth away wrath: but grievous words stir up anger. Volume is rarely the missing ingredient in a fight. The whisper de-escalates what the shout could not.

items.title: Eyes in Every Place
items.text: The eyes of the LORD are in every place, beholding the evil and the good. There is no off-camera with God. Live each moment as if it's broadcast — because to Him, it is.

items.title: A Wholesome Tongue
items.text: A wholesome tongue is a tree of life: but perverseness therein is a breach in the spirit. Words can grow gardens or punch holes. Plant; don't puncture.

items.title: Treasure in the Righteous House
items.text: In the house of the righteous is much treasure: but in the revenues of the wicked is trouble. Big paycheck plus broken home equals net loss. Quiet integrity quietly compounds.

items.title: His Delight Is the Prayer
items.text: The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination to the LORD: but the prayer of the upright is His delight. The shortest prayer of the righteous outweighs the grandest ritual of the false. Walk straight; pray simply.

items.title: A Merry Heart
items.text: A merry heart maketh a cheerful countenance: but by sorrow of the heart the spirit is broken. Inner condition tells on the face within seconds. Tend the heart and the mirror will thank you.

items.title: Better a Little With Fear
items.text: Better is little with the fear of the LORD than great treasure and trouble therewith. A small home with peace beats a mansion with a feud. Add up the right line items.

items.title: Without Counsel
items.text: Without counsel purposes are disappointed: but in the multitude of counsellors they are established. The lone-wolf entrepreneur is also the lone-wolf failure statistic. Build a council; build a future.

items.title: The House of the Proud
items.text: The LORD will destroy the house of the proud: but He will establish the border of the widow. God is in the demolition business and the construction business at the same address. He levels arrogance and lifts the forgotten.

items.title: Commit Thy Works
items.text: Commit thy works unto the LORD, and thy thoughts shall be established. The hand-off comes before the breakthrough. Hand it over each morning; receive clarity by lunch.

items.title: All Things for Himself
items.text: The LORD hath made all things for Himself: yea, even the wicked for the day of evil. Nothing slips off the table of providence. Even the chaos has a chair assigned to it.

items.title: Mercy and Truth Purge
items.text: By mercy and truth iniquity is purged: and by the fear of the LORD men depart from evil. Reverence isn't optional decoration; it's the engine that keeps a man clean.

items.title: When Your Ways Please
items.text: When a man's ways please the LORD, He maketh even his enemies to be at peace with him. Peace with people often begins with peace with God. Repair the vertical and the horizontal often heals.

items.title: Better Little With Righteousness
items.text: Better is a little with righteousness than great revenues without right. The receipt that buys you peace at night is worth more than the receipt that bought you the car.

items.title: A Crown of Glory
items.text: The hoary head is a crown of glory, if it be found in the way of righteousness. Gray hair earned in faithfulness is the only crown most people will get. Wear it well; spend it generously on the young.

items.title: Greater Than the Conqueror
items.text: He that is slow to anger is better than the mighty; and he that ruleth his spirit than he that taketh a city. The hardest territory to conquer is the six inches between your ears. Win there first.

items.title: Better a Dry Morsel
items.text: Better is a dry morsel, and quietness therewith, than an house full of sacrifices with strife. Filet mignon at a war zone is worse than a sandwich in peace. Choose the menu by the music in the room.

items.title: A Friend Loves at All Times
items.text: A friend loveth at all times, and a brother is born for adversity. Fair-weather friends evaporate; real ones materialize when the storm hits. Be the kind of friend you wish you had at 2 a.m.

items.title: He That Loveth Strife
items.text: He loveth transgression that loveth strife: and he that exalteth his gate seeketh destruction. Some people don't argue because of an issue — they argue because they love arguing. Recognize the pattern in others; recognize it in yourself.

items.title: A Merry Heart Doeth Good
items.text: A merry heart doeth good like a medicine: but a broken spirit drieth the bones. Joy is a prescription with no side effects. Laugh today; you'll feel it in your knees tomorrow.

items.title: He That Spareth Words
items.text: He that hath knowledge spareth his words: and a man of understanding is of an excellent spirit. The smartest person in the room is usually the quietest. Listening is a form of intelligence we keep underrating.

items.title: Even a Fool
items.text: Even a fool, when he holdeth his peace, is counted wise. Silence is the cheapest dignity available to anyone. Spend it freely.

items.title: Children's Children
items.text: Children's children are the crown of old men; and the glory of children are their fathers. Two generations down, two generations up — these are the medals of a faithful life. Live for the long shot.

items.title: He That Separateth Himself
items.text: Through desire a man, having separated himself, seeketh and intermeddleth with all wisdom. The lone wolf usually fancies himself wiser than the pack. Isolation is the birthplace of strange ideas.

items.title: A Fool's Delight
items.text: A fool hath no delight in understanding, but that his heart may discover itself. The fool isn't trying to learn; he's trying to broadcast. Listen for the difference in conversations.

items.title: Words as Deep Waters
items.text: The words of a man's mouth are as deep waters, and the wellspring of wisdom as a flowing brook. Every conversation is either a deep well or a flowing stream. Drink from the right ones.

items.title: A Tower You Can Run To
items.text: The name of the LORD is a strong tower: the righteous runneth into it, and is safe. Notice the verb — he runs. Refuge is offered to those who actually move toward it.

items.title: A High Wall in Imagination
items.text: The rich man's wealth is his strong city, and as an high wall in his own conceit. Money builds walls only the imagination can see. Stress-test your tower; storms ignore imaginary walls.

items.title: Answering Before Hearing
items.text: He that answereth a matter before he heareth it, it is folly and shame unto him. Half the arguments online are people responding to messages they didn't read. Be the rare reader before you become a writer.

items.title: Spirit Will Sustain
items.text: The spirit of a man will sustain his infirmity; but a wounded spirit who can bear? Bones heal faster than hearts. Nurse the inner life as carefully as you'd nurse a fracture.

items.title: A Man's Gift
items.text: A man's gift maketh room for him, and bringeth him before great men. Develop your gift in obscurity and it will introduce you in time. Skill opens doors that strings cannot.

items.title: He That Is First
items.text: He that is first in his own cause seemeth just; but his neighbour cometh and searcheth him. Every story has at least two sides. Withhold judgment until both sides have spoken.

items.title: Whoso Findeth a Wife
items.text: Whoso findeth a wife findeth a good thing, and obtaineth favour of the LORD. The right wife is not earned by deserving; she is given by grace. Treat her like the gift she is.

items.title: Better the Poor Honest
items.text: Better is the poor that walketh in his integrity, than he that is perverse in his lips, and is a fool. Empty pockets and a clean conscience beat full pockets and a rotten one. Calculate net worth on the right ledger.

items.title: Hasty Feet, Wrong Roads
items.text: Without knowledge the soul is not good; and he that hasteth with his feet sinneth. Speed without direction is the recipe for spectacular regret. Slow down; aim well.

items.title: Wealth Maketh Many Friends
items.text: Wealth maketh many friends; but the poor is separated from his neighbour. Crowds at the top of the mountain; quiet at the bottom. Cultivate the friends who would visit you in either valley.

items.title: Defer Your Anger
items.text: The discretion of a man deferreth his anger; and it is his glory to pass over a transgression. Some offenses you don't address — you absorb. The mature soul has practiced shock absorbers.

items.title: A Prudent Wife From the LORD
items.text: House and riches are the inheritance of fathers: and a prudent wife is from the LORD. You can shop for furniture; you cannot shop for character. Pray for what only God can give.

items.title: Lending to the LORD
items.text: He that hath pity upon the poor lendeth unto the LORD; and that which he hath given will He pay him again. Charity is not subtraction in God's accounting — it is investment. The yield is reliable.

items.title: Chasten While Hope
items.text: Chasten thy son while there is hope, and let not thy soul spare for his crying. The window for shaping a child closes faster than parents think. Don't trade five years of discipline for fifty years of regret.

items.title: His Counsel Shall Stand
items.text: There are many devices in a man's heart; nevertheless the counsel of the LORD, that shall stand. Make plans, but hold them with open hands. The Architect has the final blueprint.

items.title: Fear Tendeth to Life
items.text: The fear of the LORD tendeth to life: and he that hath it shall abide satisfied. The fear of God doesn't shrink your life; it ripens it. Reverence is the soil contentment grows in.

items.title: Wine Is a Mocker
items.text: Wine is a mocker, strong drink is raging: and whosoever is deceived thereby is not wise. The bottle never tells the truth in advance. The morning after is the only honest review.

items.title: Honor to Cease From Strife
items.text: It is an honour for a man to cease from strife: but every fool will be meddling. Walking away from a fight isn't weakness — it's a medal. Stop confusing endurance with maturity.

items.title: The Sluggard's Empty Field
items.text: The sluggard will not plow by reason of the cold; therefore shall he beg in harvest, and have nothing. Excuses cost more in the long run than the work would have. Plow in the cold; eat in the warmth.

items.title: Counsel as Deep Water
items.text: Counsel in the heart of man is like deep water; but a man of understanding will draw it out. Wisdom is often hidden in shy people. The right questions lower a bucket into deep wells.

items.title: Children Blessed After Him
items.text: The just man walketh in his integrity: his children are blessed after him. Your children inherit your spreadsheet but they live off your character. Leave the latter generously.

items.title: Ear and Eye Made
items.text: The hearing ear, and the seeing eye, the LORD hath made even both of them. Don't waste organs God designed. Use eyes for noticing, ears for understanding.

items.title: Love Not Sleep
items.text: Love not sleep, lest thou come to poverty; open thine eyes, and thou shalt be satisfied with bread. Bed is a friend you should not over-visit. Open the eyes; bread will appear.

items.title: Don't Meddle With the Flatterer
items.text: He that goeth about as a talebearer revealeth secrets: therefore meddle not with him that flattereth with his lips. The friend who gossips to you will gossip about you. Trust patterns, not promises.

items.title: His Lamp Put Out
items.text: Whoso curseth his father or his mother, his lamp shall be put out in obscure darkness. Disrespect to parents drains a future. Honor them — even imperfect ones — and your lamp burns longer.

items.title: Rivers of Water
items.text: The king's heart is in the hand of the LORD, as the rivers of water: He turneth it whithersoever He will. Rulers feel powerful; God redirects them like irrigation. Pray boldly for leaders — their hearts are streams in His hand.

items.title: Every Way Right in His Own Eyes
items.text: Every way of a man is right in his own eyes: but the LORD pondereth the hearts. We rate ourselves on intentions; God grades on the heart. Ask Him for an honest report.

items.title: Better Than Sacrifice
items.text: To do justice and judgment is more acceptable to the LORD than sacrifice. God prefers a fair Tuesday to a flashy Sunday. Live the offering before you bring one.

items.title: Thoughts of the Diligent
items.text: The thoughts of the diligent tend only to plenteousness; but of every one that is hasty only to want. Slow planning beats fast scrambling. Spreadsheets save what hustle alone cannot.

items.title: A Corner of the Roof
items.text: It is better to dwell in a corner of the housetop, than with a brawling woman in a wide house. Square footage cannot fix what character broke. Pick the small attic with peace.

items.title: Cry Unheard
items.text: Whoso stoppeth his ears at the cry of the poor, he also shall cry himself, but shall not be heard. The cries you ignore become the cries you make. Open your ears now; you'll need open ears later.

items.title: He That Loveth Pleasure
items.text: He that loveth pleasure shall be a poor man: he that loveth wine and oil shall not be rich. Indulgence has a price tag with small print. Love discipline more than dessert.

items.title: Better in the Wilderness
items.text: It is better to dwell in the wilderness, than with a contentious and an angry woman. Strife at home turns palaces into prisons. Tend the home before you decorate it.

items.title: Treasure in the Wise House
items.text: There is treasure to be desired and oil in the dwelling of the wise; but a foolish man spendeth it up. Wise people leave a margin; fools live in the red. Save quietly; sleep soundly.

items.title: No Wisdom Against the LORD
items.text: There is no wisdom nor understanding nor counsel against the LORD. Brilliant strategy aimed at God breaks itself on Him. Save your cleverness for the right side of history.

items.title: A Good Name
items.text: A good name is rather to be chosen than great riches, and loving favour rather than silver and gold. Reputation outlives bank accounts. Be careful what you trade for it; the resale market is brutal.

items.title: Rich and Poor Meet
items.text: The rich and poor meet together: the LORD is the maker of them all. The same God made the suit and the work boots. Treat both kinds of customer accordingly.

items.title: The Prudent Foreseeth
items.text: A prudent man foreseeth the evil, and hideth himself: but the simple pass on, and are punished. Wisdom is mostly a forecast. Read the sky now; you'll save the suitcase later.

items.title: By Humility and Fear
items.text: By humility and the fear of the LORD are riches, and honour, and life. The pathway to the things people kill for is the road most refuse to walk. Try the back stairs.

items.title: Train Up a Child
items.text: Train up a child in the way he should go: and when he is old, he will not depart from it. The grooves you cut in childhood are the rails the adult rolls on. Engineer the track early.

items.title: Borrower Is Servant
items.text: The rich ruleth over the poor, and the borrower is servant to the lender. Debt rents your future to someone else. Pay it off; reclaim the calendar.

items.title: He That Hath a Bountiful Eye
items.text: He that hath a bountiful eye shall be blessed; for he giveth of his bread to the poor. Generosity starts in the eyes — noticing before giving. Train your gaze; the wallet will follow.

items.title: Cast Out the Scorner
items.text: Cast out the scorner, and contention shall go out; yea, strife and reproach shall cease. One mocker can poison a whole table. Sometimes peace requires asking someone to leave the room.

items.title: Put a Knife to Your Throat
items.text: And put a knife to thy throat, if thou be a man given to appetite. Strong language for a strong problem. Treat appetite like an ambush, not a friend.

items.title: Labour Not to Be Rich
items.text: Labour not to be rich: cease from thine own wisdom. The pursuit of wealth as the main project is the surest way to wake up empty. Aim at God; let the rest follow.

items.title: Wings Like an Eagle
items.text: For riches certainly make themselves wings; they fly away as an eagle toward heaven. The bigger the bird, the farther it flies. Don't tie your soul to feathers.

items.title: An Evil Eye's Bread
items.text: Eat thou not the bread of him that hath an evil eye. The free meal from the wrong host is the most expensive dinner. Read the room before you reach for the menu.

items.title: Buy the Truth, Sell It Not
items.text: Buy the truth, and sell it not; also wisdom, and instruction, and understanding. Truth has a price tag and no return policy. Once you have it, never trade it for comfort.

items.title: The Father of the Righteous
items.text: The father of the righteous shall greatly rejoice: and he that begetteth a wise child shall have joy of him. The deepest parental wealth is grown children walking with God. Aim every dinner table conversation at it.

items.title: Give Me Thine Heart
items.text: My son, give me thine heart, and let thine eyes observe my ways. The Father is not after performance — He's after the heart. Hand it over; the rest sorts itself.

items.title: Look Not on the Wine
items.text: Look not thou upon the wine when it is red, when it giveth his colour in the cup. The eye is the first organ to drink. Look away early; the rest of the body listens.

items.title: At the Last It Biteth
items.text: At the last it biteth like a serpent, and stingeth like an adder. The bottle smiles in the bar and bites in the bedroom. The end is the only honest part of the conversation.

items.title: Through Wisdom a House Is Builded
