items.text: Through wisdom is an house builded; and by understanding it is established. Architects of homes need more than blueprints — they need wisdom. Build with the right material in every room.

items.title: A Wise Man Is Strong
items.text: A wise man is strong; yea, a man of knowledge increaseth strength. Brawn fades; brains compound. Invest in the muscle that survives the gym closing.

items.title: Counsellors and Safety
items.text: For by wise counsel thou shalt make thy war: and in the multitude of counsellors there is safety. Even generals get advisors. Whatever battle you're in, recruit the right voices before the first move.

items.title: Strength Is Small
items.text: If thou faint in the day of adversity, thy strength is small. Adversity doesn't manufacture weakness; it reveals it. Train in peace for the fight you can't see coming.

items.title: Drawn Unto Death
items.text: If thou forbear to deliver them that are drawn unto death, and those that are ready to be slain. Some neighbors are walking the plank quietly. Step in; God notices the silence as much as the rescue.

items.title: Honey Is Good
items.text: My son, eat thou honey, because it is good... so shall the knowledge of wisdom be unto thy soul. Wisdom is sweet to the soul as honey is to the tongue. Make daily meals of it.

items.title: Falleth Seven Times
items.text: For a just man falleth seven times, and riseth up again. Counting the falls is for accountants; counting the get-ups is for saints. Get up.

items.title: Rejoice Not When He Falleth
items.text: Rejoice not when thine enemy falleth, and let not thine heart be glad when he stumbleth. Schadenfreude is a window into your own future failure. Pray for those you'd otherwise celebrate seeing humbled.

items.title: The Field of the Slothful
items.text: I went by the field of the slothful... and, lo, it was all grown over with thorns. Neglect is its own gardener — it always grows weeds. What field of yours needs ten minutes today?

items.title: The Glory of Kings
items.text: It is the glory of God to conceal a thing: but the honour of kings is to search out a matter. God hides; rulers seek. Be a digger, not a skimmer — the deepest answers reward the patient.

items.title: Take Away the Dross
items.text: Take away the dross from the silver, and there shall come forth a vessel for the finer. Refining looks like loss; it is preparation. The vessel God uses tomorrow goes through the fire today.

items.title: Stand Not in the Place of Great Men
items.text: Put not forth thyself in the presence of the king, and stand not in the place of great men. Pushing for the seat of honor usually pushes you out of it. Take the back row; let them call you forward.

items.title: Debate Thy Cause
items.text: Debate thy cause with thy neighbour himself; and discover not a secret to another. Take it to him, not to the group chat. Most relationships die of triangulation.

items.title: Withdraw Thy Foot
items.text: Withdraw thy foot from thy neighbour's house; lest he be weary of thee, and so hate thee. Even friendship has occupancy limits. Be the guest who leaves a little early; be welcomed back.

items.title: City Without Walls
items.text: He that hath no rule over his own spirit is like a city that is broken down, and without walls. Self-control is municipal infrastructure. Without it, every passerby can loot you.

items.title: Honour Not Seemly
items.text: As snow in summer, and as rain in harvest, so honour is not seemly for a fool. Promote the wrong man and the whole season suffers. Promotions are not awards — they are weather changes.

items.title: The Curse Causeless
items.text: The curse causeless shall not come. Sticks and stones break bones; baseless curses break nothing. Don't fear words spoken without ground in heaven.

items.title: A Rod for the Fool's Back
items.text: A whip for the horse, a bridle for the ass, and a rod for the fool's back. Each creature responds to the right tool. Don't reason with what is meant to be restrained.

items.title: Answer Not a Fool
items.text: Answer not a fool according to his folly, lest thou also be like unto him. Some arguments lower you by participation. Walk away with your dignity intact.

items.title: A Thorn in a Drunkard's Hand
items.text: As a thorn goeth up into the hand of a drunkard, so is a parable in the mouth of fools. Profound truth quoted by a fool becomes a weapon — first to others, eventually to himself. Quote carefully; live carefully.

items.title: As a Dog Returneth
items.text: As a dog returneth to his vomit, so a fool returneth to his folly. Repeat offenders are not surprised by their failures — they're addicted to them. Break the loop or it will break you.

items.title: Where No Wood Is
items.text: Where no wood is, there the fire goeth out: so where there is no talebearer, the strife ceaseth. Most fires need fuel to last. Stop carrying logs; the conflict goes cold.

items.title: Whoso Diggeth a Pit
items.text: Whoso diggeth a pit shall fall therein: and he that rolleth a stone, it will return upon him. Schemes have a habit of looping back to the schemer. Stop digging holes for others; you'll save yourself a fall.

items.title: Boast Not of Tomorrow
items.text: Boast not thyself of tomorrow; for thou knowest not what a day may bring forth. Tomorrow is not on your calendar yet — it is on God's. Plan with humility; speak with reservations.

items.title: Let Another Praise Thee
items.text: Let another man praise thee, and not thine own mouth; a stranger, and not thine own lips. Self-promotion costs you the very honor you sought. Let your work talk; close your mouth.

items.title: Faithful Wounds
items.text: Faithful are the wounds of a friend; but the kisses of an enemy are deceitful. The friend who tells you the hard truth is more loyal than the flatterer who agrees with everything. Choose the wound over the kiss.

items.title: A Bird Wandering
items.text: As a bird that wandereth from her nest, so is a man that wandereth from his place. Out of place, even strong men weaken. Stay where God plants you; the nest is the strategy.

items.title: Iron Sharpens Iron
items.text: Iron sharpeneth iron; so a man sharpeneth the countenance of his friend. The friction is not the failure of friendship; it is the function. Pick friends who file you, not flatter you.

items.title: Heart Answereth Heart
items.text: As in water face answereth to face, so the heart of man to man. We are mirrors of each other. The heart you bring to a room shapes the hearts you meet there.

items.title: Know Your Flocks
items.text: Be thou diligent to know the state of thy flocks, and look well to thy herds. The shepherd who doesn't know his sheep loses them one at a time. Audit what God put in your care.

items.title: Bold as a Lion
items.text: The wicked flee when no man pursueth: but the righteous are bold as a lion. Guilt invents pursuers; integrity sleeps with the door open. Boldness is the byproduct of a clean conscience.

items.title: Many Princes
items.text: For the transgression of a land many are the princes thereof: but by a man of understanding... the state thereof shall be prolonged. Stable nations need stable men. Pray for understanding leaders before structural ones.

items.title: Cover or Confess
items.text: He that covereth his sins shall not prosper: but whoso confesseth and forsaketh them shall have mercy. Cover-ups age badly; confession ages into freedom. Choose the briefly painful path.

items.title: Feareth Always
items.text: Happy is the man that feareth alway: but he that hardeneth his heart shall fall into mischief. A tender heart bends; a hard one breaks. Keep yours soft toward God.

items.title: He That Maketh Haste
items.text: A faithful man shall abound with blessings: but he that maketh haste to be rich shall not be innocent. Speed and integrity rarely cohabit. Pick faithfulness; the blessings show up on their own schedule.

items.title: For a Piece of Bread
items.text: To have respect of persons is not good: for for a piece of bread that man will transgress. Cheap bribes corrupt big offices. Notice what you'd compromise for; price yourself out of the market.

items.title: Trusteth in His Own Heart
items.text: He that trusteth in his own heart is a fool: but whoso walketh wisely, he shall be delivered. Your heart is a roommate, not a king. Listen to it; don't enthrone it.

items.title: He That Giveth
items.text: He that giveth unto the poor shall not lack: but he that hideth his eyes shall have many a curse. The hidden eye costs more than the open hand. Open both today.

items.title: A Sweeping Rain
items.text: A poor man that oppresseth the poor is like a sweeping rain which leaveth no food. The cruelest oppressor is sometimes the one who knows the pain personally. Heal forward, not sideways.

items.title: He That Keepeth the Law
items.text: Whoso keepeth the law is a wise son: but he that is a companion of riotous men shameth his father. Companions you choose write checks your parents cash in shame. Pick friends like you'd pick co-signers.

items.title: Often Reproved
items.text: He, that being often reproved hardeneth his neck, shall suddenly be destroyed, and that without remedy. There is a last warning. You don't hear it labeled — you hear it later, when the door is shut.

items.title: When Righteous Are in Authority
items.text: When the righteous are in authority, the people rejoice: but when the wicked beareth rule, the people mourn. Mood of a country tracks the morals of its leaders. Vote for joy.

items.title: Whoso Loveth Wisdom
items.text: Whoso loveth wisdom rejoiceth his father: but he that keepeth company with harlots spendeth his substance. Bank statements tell the spiritual story. Audit yours like a father would.

items.title: A Net for His Feet
items.text: A man that flattereth his neighbour spreadeth a net for his feet. Flattery is a hunting tool, not a love language. Compliments without truth are bait, not blessing.

items.title: All His Mind
items.text: A fool uttereth all his mind: but a wise man keepeth it in till afterwards. Not every thought needs to be a tweet. Many opinions ripen in silence and rot in publication.

items.title: Where No Vision Is
items.text: Where there is no vision, the people perish: but he that keepeth the law, happy is he. Vision is not a corporate retreat exercise; it is prophetic clarity. Pray for it; obey what you see.

items.title: Rod and Reproof
items.text: The rod and reproof give wisdom: but a child left to himself bringeth his mother to shame. Loving correction now prevents devastating regret later. Don't outsource the rod or the reproof.

items.title: Fear of Man Bringeth a Snare
items.text: The fear of man bringeth a snare: but whoso putteth his trust in the LORD shall be safe. Approval-addiction is its own prison. Fear God and the human jury loses its grip.

items.title: Judgment Cometh From the LORD
items.text: Many seek the ruler's favour; but every man's judgment cometh from the LORD. Networking is no substitute for righteousness. The final verdict isn't issued in the corner office.

items.title: Every Word Pure
items.text: Every word of God is pure: He is a shield unto them that put their trust in Him. Refine the Bible all you want — it comes out the same. Stand under it; it stands under you.

items.title: Add Thou Not
items.text: Add thou not unto His words, lest He reprove thee, and thou be found a liar. The temptation to improve Scripture is older than printing. Resist editing what was given complete.

items.title: Two Things Have I Required
items.text: Two things have I required of thee... give me neither poverty nor riches; feed me with food convenient for me. The wisest prayer is the middle prayer. Both extremes have their own ways of damaging the soul.

items.title: Lest I Deny Thee
items.text: Lest I be full, and deny Thee, and say, Who is the LORD? Comfort makes amnesia. Pray for the kind of memory that survives blessing.

items.title: The Eye That Mocketh
items.text: The eye that mocketh at his father, and despiseth to obey his mother, the ravens of the valley shall pick it out. The God of nature avenges the dishonor of parents. Reverence both while you can.

items.title: Three Things the Earth Cannot Bear
items.text: For three things the earth is disquieted... For a servant when he reigneth; and a fool when he is filled with meat; and... an odious woman when she is married. Promotions to the unworthy unsettle whole societies. Pray for character before influence.

items.title: Little but Exceeding Wise
items.text: There be four things which are little upon the earth, but they are exceeding wise. Size is not strategy. The ant, the coney, the locust, and the spider out-think creatures fifty times bigger.

items.title: Not for Kings to Drink
items.text: It is not for kings, O Lemuel, it is not for kings to drink wine; nor for princes strong drink. Leadership doesn't have the luxury of dulled judgment. Stay sober for the people you lead.

items.title: Open Thy Mouth for the Dumb
items.text: Open thy mouth for the dumb in the cause of all such as are appointed to destruction. Some people have no voice in the room where decisions are made. Be theirs.

items.title: Far Above Rubies
items.text: Who can find a virtuous woman? for her price is far above rubies. The rare jewel of Scripture is not a stone but a soul. Marry one; raise one; be one.

items.title: Heart of Her Husband Trusteth
items.text: The heart of her husband doth safely trust in her, so that he shall have no need of spoil. Trust is the cheapest luxury and the rarest. Cultivate the kind of marriage where neither needs to verify.

items.title: She Riseth While It Is Yet Night
items.text: She riseth also while it is yet night, and giveth meat to her household. The household ahead of the calendar starts before the sun does. Quiet hours build loud blessings.

items.title: She Considereth a Field
items.text: She considereth a field, and buyeth it: with the fruit of her hands she planteth a vineyard. The virtuous woman is also a shrewd steward. Domestic doesn't mean uneconomic.

items.title: She Stretcheth Out Her Hand
items.text: She stretcheth out her hand to the poor; yea, she reacheth forth her hands to the needy. Her productivity has a destination beyond her own household. Wealth in godly hands flows outward.

items.title: Strength and Honour
items.text: Strength and honour are her clothing; and she shall rejoice in time to come. The best wardrobe is woven from character. Other fabrics fade; this one looks better with age.

items.title: Law of Kindness
items.text: She openeth her mouth with wisdom; and in her tongue is the law of kindness. Notice the pairing: wisdom and kindness in the same sentence. Either alone is incomplete.

items.title: Her Children Arise
items.text: Her children arise up, and call her blessed; her husband also, and he praiseth her. The standing ovation of a faithful family is the only awards show that matters. Live for the curtain call at home.

items.title: Favour Is Deceitful
items.text: Favour is deceitful, and beauty is vain: but a woman that feareth the LORD, she shall be praised. Looks fade; reverence ripens. Praise what God praises; you'll praise the right things.
